title: Shin Eva leak general
wikitext:
[]: Shin Eva leak general Post by Velorex » Fri Mar 05, 2021 7:52 pm Original Title: 3+1 SUBTITLES AVAILABLE FOR DOWNLOAD WITH APP!- Blockio According to Toho's website they have a app available to download the subtitles for 3+1 https://theater.toho.co.jp/toho_theaterlist/shin-evangelion.html Anyone have any idea what this is?

[]: Re: Download JP subtitles with app? Post by Pluto » Fri Mar 05, 2021 8:40 pm Thanks for the post! I downloaded the app. It seems you will need headphones for the audio or smart glasses for the subtitles. However, I'm not sure if you will be able to use smart glasses provided by the theater.

[]: Re: Download JP subtitles with app? Post by EvaThirdImpact » Fri Mar 05, 2021 9:23 pm Hmmm, maybe someone could find a way around the glasses and be able to pull the subtitles from this. I'm assuming that would make the fansub a lot easier. I also know absolutely nothing about what it takes to sub something so I could be mistaken.

[]: Re: Download JP subtitles with app? Post by Velorex » Fri Mar 05, 2021 10:08 pm idk dude

[]: Re: 3+1 SUBTITLES AVAILABLE FOR DOWNLOAD WITH APP! Post by Sailor Star Dust » Sat Mar 06, 2021 12:44 am My understanding is these are Japanese subtitles only , since the JP Eva blurays even have that as a bonus option. It's maybe possible some of the theatrical screenings would offer English subtitles, but I haven't read that anywhere.

[]: Re: 3+1 SUBTITLES AVAILABLE FOR DOWNLOAD WITH APP! Post by Thesufferingpumpkin » Sat Mar 06, 2021 2:31 am I love the irony that the app name is written in English though lol

[]: Re: 3+1 SUBTITLES AVAILABLE FOR DOWNLOAD WITH APP! Post by Thesufferingpumpkin » Sat Mar 06, 2021 7:33 am A source close to the matter has told me the original plan was that as the original release was in the time of the Tokyo olympics, due to the large presence of international travellers there at one time, there would of been numerous showings of shin eva with different language subtitles. Sadly not to be

[]: Re: 3+1 SUBTITLES AVAILABLE FOR DOWNLOAD WITH APP! Post by Cookie H Wilson » Sat Mar 06, 2021 7:42 am Downloaded the app for iOS How do I use it? I’ve searched in coming soon input in the search box “シン・エヴァンゲリオン劇場版 “ But I can’t seem to understand how it works or find the movie link Somebody can help me out with this

[]: Re: 3+1 SUBTITLES AVAILABLE FOR DOWNLOAD WITH APP! Post by EvaThirdImpact » Sat Mar 06, 2021 4:37 pm Cookie H Wilson wrote: Downloaded the app for iOS How do I use it? I’ve searched in coming soon input in the search box “シン・エヴァンゲリオン劇場版 “ But I can’t seem to understand how it works or find the movie link Somebody can help me out with this

[]: Re: 3+1 SUBTITLES AVAILABLE FOR DOWNLOAD WITH APP! Post by SEELE-01 » Sat Mar 06, 2021 11:21 pm Thesufferingpumpkin wrote: I love the irony that the app name is written in English though lol

[]: Re: 3+1 SUBTITLES AVAILABLE FOR DOWNLOAD WITH APP! Post by kuro hazama » Sun Mar 07, 2021 11:13 am someone got the audio descriptions as a .dat file apparently, subtitles may be inclued in it

[]: Re: 3+1 SUBTITLES AVAILABLE FOR DOWNLOAD WITH APP! Post by hui43210 » Sun Mar 07, 2021 11:22 am kuro hazama wrote: someone got the audio descriptions as a .dat file apparently, subtitles may be inclued in it

[]: Re: 3+1 SUBTITLES AVAILABLE FOR DOWNLOAD WITH APP! Post by Tajai » Sun Mar 07, 2021 11:24 am kuro hazama wrote: someone got the audio descriptions as a .dat file apparently, subtitles may be inclued in it

[]: Re: 3+1 SUBTITLES AVAILABLE FOR DOWNLOAD WITH APP! Post by duppertip » Sun Mar 07, 2021 11:27 am We need a separate thread for those leaks, don't we? Or will it be fine to discuss here?

[]: Re: 3+1 SUBTITLES AVAILABLE FOR DOWNLOAD WITH APP! Post by Blockio » Sun Mar 07, 2021 11:34 am Looks like we won't be needing anything of the like, as the people working on it have run into a brick wall in the shape of missing authentification. As regrettable as this is, it was very fulfilling to see those idiots on 4chan hyping themselves up as "we can't stop winning" only to realize that their supposed "leak" was someone posting a transcript of EoE.

[]: Re: 3+1 SUBTITLES AVAILABLE FOR DOWNLOAD WITH APP! Post by kuro hazama » Sun Mar 07, 2021 12:15 pm Ok data´s ripped but Ive got no idea how or with what to interpret/execute the dat file.

[]: Re: 3+1 SUBTITLES AVAILABLE FOR DOWNLOAD WITH APP! Post by Heaven Piercing Man » Sun Mar 07, 2021 12:19 pm Can't they be decrypted through "going to watch the film"?

[]: Re: 3+1 SUBTITLES AVAILABLE FOR DOWNLOAD WITH APP! Post by Blockio » Sun Mar 07, 2021 1:03 pm Looks like user blue on the discord has made the breakthrough with the ideas presented by Velorex , recording the guide for the visually impaired as we speak. In other words: A detailed, spoken description of everything that happens in the movie. No troll spoilers this time around.

[]: Re: 3+1 SUBTITLES AVAILABLE FOR DOWNLOAD WITH APP! Post by kuro hazama » Sun Mar 07, 2021 1:08 pm Except dialogues, right?

[]: Re: 3+1 SUBTITLES AVAILABLE FOR DOWNLOAD WITH APP! Post by Blockio » Sun Mar 07, 2021 1:09 pm Yeah, but I'm fairly positive that they'll be able to get their hands on that, too. If one is accessible, I don't see why the other shouldn't be EDIT: Might not be possible after all. In blue's own words, "just download the hello movie app, play the avant thing from youtube and hold your phone to the speaker". Since the subtitle track requires a seperate device, it might run over a different system; unsure about that. (Anyone who has a set of smart glasses is more than welcome to try that method out tho!) EDIT2: The process, as stated by blue: just download the hello movie app, play the avant thing from youtube and hold your phone to the speaker all i am doing is letting the app do what it is supposed to do yes, once it started it doesn't end yes it is accurate, because everything said lines up with the first 12 minutes (my Japanese is good enough to understand ritsuko and "eva doing acrobatics") i got my phone hooked up as a "microphone" to my laptop right now so we'll end up with a two hour long file of a man explaining what's seen i did manage to extract the file itself, but no way how to play it on my pc, so this is the only way to anyone who wants to try this: you have to set the app language to Japanese in its settings for eva to pop up also, the audio will not play through the phone speakers, only when an "earpiece" is connected, so a phone with a headphone jack would be useful, i just used a regular ass headset

[]: Re: Download JP subtitles with app? Post by Pluto » Fri Mar 05, 2021 8:40 pm Thanks for the post! I downloaded the app. It seems you will need headphones
